description: Claude Code Usage HUD - Electron system tray app showing plan usage limits with progress bars and reset countdowns

userStories.title: Electron app scaffold with system tray
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a minimal Electron app that launches into the system tray so the HUD runs as a lightweight background app.
acceptanceCriteria: Electron app bootstrapped with main process and renderer
Package.json has 'start' and 'build' scripts
App launches minimized to system tray (no taskbar entry, no visible window on start)
Tray icon uses a built-in Electron nativeImage or a bundled PNG icon
Right-click tray icon shows context menu with 'Show', 'Refresh', 'Quit' options
Clicking 'Quit' exits the app cleanly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use app.requestSingleInstanceLock() from the start to prevent duplicate instances

userStories.title: Floating overlay window
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click the tray icon to see a compact floating overlay so I can check my usage at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Left-clicking the tray icon toggles a frameless, always-on-top BrowserWindow
Overlay appears positioned near the system tray (bottom-right on Windows)
Overlay is approximately 320px wide with adaptive height
Overlay has dark theme background with rounded corners and subtle drop shadow
Overlay closes when pressing Escape
Overlay closes when it loses focus (click outside)
Overlay window has a basic HTML page with placeholder text 'Usage data loading...'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use BrowserWindow with frame:false, transparent:true, alwaysOnTop:true, skipTaskbar:true

userStories.title: Usage data fetching service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service in the main process that reads Claude Code usage data so the overlay can display it.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a usageService module in the main process
Service reads usage data from ~/.claude/ local files or parses 'claude usage' CLI output
Service returns a structured object: { models: [{ name, used, limit, resetTime }], session: { used, limit } }
If data source is unavailable, service returns null without throwing
Service is callable from main process and exposes data via IPC to renderer
Add IPC handler: 'get-usage-data' that renderer can invoke
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Investigate ~/.claude/ directory for cached usage data first. Fall back to parsing CLI output of 'claude usage' if needed.

userStories.title: Render per-model usage progress bars
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my usage per model as color-coded progress bars with counts so I know how close I am to limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay renderer calls 'get-usage-data' IPC on load and displays results
Each model (Opus, Sonnet, Haiku) shown as a labeled row with model name
Each row has a progress bar that fills proportionally to used/limit ratio
Progress bar color: green when <60%, yellow when 60-85%, red when >85%
Each row shows 'X / Y' text next to the bar (e.g., '142 / 200')
If data is unavailable, show 'Unable to fetch usage data' message
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use CSS custom properties for colors. Keep styling inline or in a single CSS file bundled with the renderer.

userStories.title: Weekly reset countdown display
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a countdown to when my weekly limits reset so I can plan my work.
acceptanceCriteria: Below the progress bars, show a 'Resets in Xd Xh Xm' countdown
Countdown updates every 60 seconds via setInterval in the renderer
Reset time is derived from the usage data returned by the service
If reset time is unknown, show 'Reset time unavailable'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Current session usage section
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my current session usage separately from weekly totals.
acceptanceCriteria: Overlay has a 'Current Session' section visually separated from 'Weekly Usage' section
Session section shows token or message count if available from the data source
If session data is unavailable, show 'Session data not available'
Clear visual divider (border or spacing) between the two sections
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Auto-refresh and manual refresh
userStories.description: As a user, I want the data to refresh automatically and on demand so I always see current usage.
acceptanceCriteria: Usage data auto-refreshes every 5 minutes via setInterval in main process
Main process sends 'usage-data-updated' event to renderer after each refresh
Overlay has a small 'Refresh' button that triggers an immediate IPC fetch
'Refresh' in the tray context menu triggers an immediate fetch and updates the overlay
Overlay shows 'Last updated: X min ago' text that updates each minute
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Single instance lock and refresh-on-relaunch
userStories.description: As a user, I want to avoid duplicate HUD instances — relaunching should just refresh the existing one.
acceptanceCriteria: App uses app.requestSingleInstanceLock() to prevent multiple instances
If a second instance is launched, the existing instance receives the 'second-instance' event
On 'second-instance' event, the app triggers a data refresh and shows the overlay
The second instance exits immediately without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This enables the Claude Code hook to 'launch' the app and have it just refresh if already running

userStories.title: Claude Code session-start hook
userStories.description: As a user, I want the HUD to auto-launch whenever I start a Claude Code CLI session.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a hook config snippet for ~/.claude/settings.json that runs on session start
Hook command launches the Electron app (e.g., 'npm start --prefix /path/to/prominence')
Hook runs in background and does not block Claude Code session startup
Document the hook setup in a README or SETUP.md file
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Claude Code hooks go in ~/.claude/settings.json under the 'hooks' key. The hook should use 'command' with background execution.
